sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient is a 6-month follow-up visit after a heart attack 6 months prior, during which a stent was placed in the left anterior descending artery.
He has been taking aspirin, brilinta, and crestor.
He reports a complaint of dizziness when standing up quickly, which he attributes to either the heart attack or medication.
He is also concerned about the number of medications he is taking.
He has a history of diabetes, which likely contributed to his heart attack.
He has been taking fish oil and reports a recent hemoglobin A1c of approximately 60.
He also has stage 3 chronic kidney disease and has not seen his nephrologist in a year.
He recently did a blood test, which showed stable kidney function.
He has a history of being a fan of the San Francisco 49ers.
Past medical history: The patient had a heart attack 6 months prior, for which a stent was placed in the left anterior descending artery.
He also has stage 3 chronic kidney disease and diabetes.
Review of systems: The patient reports dizziness when standing up quickly.
Current medications:
Aspirin
Brilinta
Crestor 40 mg once a day
Metoprolol 50 mg once a day
Fish oil
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 127/80.
Physical exam: The patient has a slight 3/6 systolic ejection murmur heard at the left base and 1+ nonpitting edema in the lower extremities.
Test Results: The patient's kidney function is stable.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has coronary artery disease, stage 3 chronic kidney disease, and diabetes.
Problem list: Coronary artery disease; stage 3 chronic kidney disease; diabetes; mild depression; difficulty adjusting after heart attack.
Plan:
I prescribed crestor 40 mg once a day for high cholesterol.
I will refill the patient's metoprolol, aspirin, and brilinta.
I will refer the patient to cardiac rehab to increase his activity level and confidence in exercising.
I will also refer the patient to the dietitian for dietary education to manage his diabetes.
I will order another hemoglobin A1c and discuss his insulin regimen with his primary care physician.
I will reach out to doctor Miller to schedule an appointment for the patient to follow up with his nephrologist.
I will place a referral to psychiatry for difficulty adjusting after heart attack and mild depression.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with the dietitian, his primary care physician, and his nephrologist as needed.
